title: Roadmap emits a false `complete` from a second completeness derivation divergent from the authoritative one (surfaces when positioned-task coverage is partial)
description:
The roadmap runs its own completeness derivation (rollupPhaseStatus) over positioned tasks (task_positioned_in_phase edges), separate from the authoritative derivedRollupComplete used by gate satisfaction, the derived-status invariant, reconcile, and converge. The two can disagree: when a phase's positioned tasks all bucket complete but its authored status is not complete, rollupPhaseStatus emits rollup: complete while the authoritative verdict — and the stored phase/milestone status — is not-complete. The roadmap view then presents {rollup: complete, status: planned} as contradictory raw data. rollupPhaseStatus is display-only (no completeness consumer reads it): the authoritative gate/invariant/reconcile path is unaffected and does not misfire; the harm is confined to roadmap consumers (LLMs, HTML views, agent context) that read the false verdict.

Triggering instance: MILE-005 / PHASE-M5-WRITE-INTEGRITY on 2026-07-08. PHASE-M5 has one positioned task (TASK-027, completed) and authored status in-progress; the roadmap derived rollup: complete while the authoritative verdict (and stored phase: in-progress / milestone: planned) was not-complete. An LLM shown {rollup: complete, status: planned} defaulted to 'stored status is stale'.
impact: Roadmap consumers (LLMs, HTML views, agent context composition) read a false completeness verdict and must manually cross-verify every roadmap completeness claim. The authoritative gate/invariant/reconcile path is correct; the defect is confined to the roadmap's independent, display-only derivation — but that verdict drives consumer decisions until completeness is single-sourced.

evidence.reference: PHASE-M5-WRITE-INTEGRITY: authored status in-progress; one positioned task (TASK-027, completed) via task_positioned_in_phase
MILE-005: stored status 'planned'; roadmap rollup bucket 'complete' — the roadmap's task-derived bucket contradicts the authoritative not-complete verdict

rollupPhaseStatus derives a completeness bucket by aggregating positioned tasks, independent of the authoritative derivedRollupComplete; the roadmap surfaces this bucket next to the status field with no single source of completeness truth

proposed_resolution: Single-source the completeness verdict. The roadmap must not run a second completeness derivation. The roadmap's milestone/phase completeness routes through the authoritative derivedRollupComplete (context-sdk.ts) — the same function gate satisfaction, the derived-status invariant, reconcile, and converge use — never rollupPhaseStatus's independent positioned-task aggregation. rollupPhaseStatus is retained only as a task-progress display, labeled as progress, never surfaced as a completeness or reached verdict, so a progress count can neither read as nor contradict the authoritative status. Scope is read only from typed edges (task_positioned_in_phase); no membership is inferred from the intent prose. With completeness single-sourced, a phase whose authored status is not complete derives not-complete regardless of positioned-task coverage, and the false complete cannot arise.
